title: Charred Grilled Malaysian Chive Rice Bowl
description: A grassy, bouncy-qq monsoon Malaysian dish featuring chopped chive and a bhuna-inspired flavor profile. Serve as a low-calorie-friendly vessel on a binchotan grill.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a pan over medium-high heat. Sear the chicken breast for 5 minutes on each side, or until cooked through.
Transfer the chicken to a plate and set aside to rest.
In the same pan, add the remaining 1 tablespoon of coconut oil. Add the chopped onion and cook for 3-4 minutes, or until softened.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly.
Stir in the bhuna curry paste and cook for 1 minute, or until fragrant.
Add the cooked white rice to the pan and stir to combine with the curry paste mixture.
Add the chopped chives, fish sauce, lime juice, salt, and pepper to the pan and stir to combine.
Slice the cooked chicken breast and place on top of the rice mixture.
